title: Missing bits in display of a concat
body:
I have an example with this:
```
$display("%x", t2);
$display("%x", { t2[15:8], 4'd0, t2[3:0] });
```
and Modelsim and Icarus output this as expected:
```
ff0f
ff0f
```
but Verilator seems to lose the upper bits in the second display:
```
ff0f
0f
```
I see that the following code was generated:
```
VL_WRITEF("%x\n%x\n",16,vlTOPp->display_concat__DOT__t2,
          8,(0xff0fU & (IData)(vlTOPp->display_concat__DOT__t2)));
```
I would guess that the `8` for the second argument is the problem.  Something must have computed the wrong size?

Here is the self-contained example: [display_concat.tar.gz](https://github.com/verilator/verilator/files/7552767/display_concat.tar.gz)
